title: Ensure that garbage collection is configured as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that garbage collection is configured as appropriate
input_row.description: Activate garbage collector on pod termination, as appropriate.
input_row.rationale: Garbage collection is important to ensure sufficient resource availability and avoiding degraded performance and availability. In the worst case, the system might crash or just be unusable for a long period of time. The current setting for garbage collection is 12,500 terminated pods which might be too high for your system to sustain. Based on your system resources and tests, choose an appropriate threshold value to activate garbage collection.
input_row.audit: Two types of garbage collection are performed on an OpenShift Container Platform node: • Container garbage collection: Removes terminated containers. • Image garbage collection: Removes images not referenced by any running pods. Container garbage collection can be performed using eviction thresholds. Image garbage collection relies on disk usage as reported by cAdvisor on the node to decide which images 144 | P a g e to remove from the node. Default values are found here https://github.com/openshift/kubernetes-kubelet/blob/origin-4.5-kubernetes-1.18.3/config/v1beta1/types.go#L554-L604 The OpenShift administrator can configure how OpenShift Container Platform performs garbage collection by creating a kubeletConfig object for each Machine Config Pool using any combination of the following: • soft eviction for containers • hard eviction for containers • eviction for images To configure, follow the directions in https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.5/nodes/nodes/nodes-nodes-garbage-collection.html#nodes-nodes-garbage-collection-configuring_nodes-nodes-configuring To verify settings, run the following command for each updated configpool oc get machineconfigpool # For each machineconfigpool oc describe machineconfigpool <name> #For example oc describe machineconfigpool master oc describe machineconfigpool worker Verify the values for the following are set as appropriate. eviction-soft evictionSoftGracePeriod evictionHard evictionPressureTransitionPeriod
input_row.remediation: To configure, follow the directions in Configuring garbage collection for containers and images
input_row.references: 145 | P a g e 1. https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.5/operators/operator- reference.html#cluster-openshift-controller-manager-operator_red-hat-operators 2. https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.5/operators/operator- reference.html#kube-controller-manager-operator_red-hat-operators 3. https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.5/nodes/nodes/nodes-nodes- garbage-collection.html 4. https://github.com/openshift/kubernetes-kubelet/blob/origin-4.5-kubernetes- 1.18.3/config/v1beta1/types.go#L554-L604 5. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube- controller-manager/ 6. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/28484
input_row.default_value: Container garbage collection is enabled by default and happens automatically in response to eviction thresholds being reached. Default values are found here https://github.com/openshift/kubernetes-kubelet/blob/origin-4.5-kubernetes-1.18.3/config/v1beta1/types.go#L554-L604

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the OpenShift Console:**
   - Navigate to the OpenShift Container Platform dashboard in your IBM Cloud account.
   - Select the project/namespace where your pods are deployed.

2. **Check Machine Config Pools:**
   - Open a terminal with access to the `oc` command-line interface (CLI).
   - Run the command to list all Machine Config Pools:
     ```
     oc get machineconfigpool
     ```

3. **Describe Each Machine Config Pool:**
   - For each machine config pool returned in the previous command, run:
     ```
     oc describe machineconfigpool <name>
     ```
   - Replace `<name>` with the name of the Machine Config Pool (e.g., `master` or `worker`).

4. **Verify Garbage Collection Settings:**
   - Check the values for the following parameters in the output:
     - `eviction-soft`
     - `evictionSoftGracePeriod`
     - `evictionHard`
     - `evictionPressureTransitionPeriod`
   - Ensure these values are set appropriately according to your system resources and performance requirements.

5. **Expected Secure Configuration:**
   - Confirm that garbage collection is configured to meet the optimal thresholds suitable for your environment, which may be lower than the default limit of 12,500 terminated pods.

6. **Document Compliance:**
   - Record the current configurations and adjustments made as part of your audit, ensuring the settings adhere to the recommended practices outlined in the documentation links provided in the audit control.

By following these steps, you can comprehensively assess whether the garbage collection in your OpenShift environment is configured correctly and appropriate for your use case.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** PARTIALLY_AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The current assessment uses a manual approach, which is partially appropriate. While manual verification is necessary for understanding and interpreting the settings, some aspects of this audit can be automated using IBM Cloud CLI and OpenShift CLI (`oc`) commands. The `oc` commands used in the manual steps can be scripted to automate the retrieval and verification of garbage collection settings.
- **IBM Cloud APIs/IBM Cloud CLI/CLI:** The IBM Cloud CLI and OpenShift CLI (`oc`) can automate the retrieval of machine config pool details. However, interpreting whether the garbage collection settings are "appropriate" might still require manual judgment based on specific environment requirements.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7

- **Automation Details:** The manual steps are technically correct and use valid `oc` commands to retrieve necessary information. However, the assessment lacks automation details that could streamline the process.
- **IBM Cloud CLI Methods:** The assessment does not leverage IBM Cloud CLI capabilities directly, but the use of `oc` commands is appropriate for OpenShift environments.
- **Manual Steps:** The manual steps are accurate and complete for checking the garbage collection settings, but they could be enhanced with automation scripts.
- **Program Name Convention:** The assessment does not specify a program name. A more descriptive name could be `ibm_openshift_garbage_collection_configuration_check`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Automation:** Introduce scripts using `oc` commands to automate the retrieval of machine config pool details and check garbage collection settings. This could reduce the manual effort and increase efficiency.
- **Missing Considerations:** The assessment should consider using IBM Cloud CLI to manage and automate tasks related to OpenShift clusters on IBM Cloud. Additionally, consider integrating with IBM Cloud Monitoring to track and alert on garbage collection metrics.
- **Alternative Methods:** Explore IBM Cloud's API capabilities for managing OpenShift clusters, which might provide more integrated solutions for configuration checks.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** Use a more descriptive name such as `ibm_openshift_garbage_collection_configuration_check` to clarify the specific security check being performed.

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- The assessment provides a solid manual approach, but the lack of automation and IBM Cloud CLI integration reduces confidence in its efficiency and scalability.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- **Modify:** Introduce automation scripts using `oc` commands to streamline the retrieval and verification process. Consider integrating IBM Cloud CLI and API capabilities where applicable to enhance the audit process. Re-evaluate the classification to include an automated component for the retrieval and initial assessment of settings, while maintaining manual judgment for appropriateness based on specific requirements.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
The task of verifying garbage collection settings on OpenShift can be automated using the OpenShift CLI (`oc`). The retrieval of machine config pool details and garbage collection settings can be scripted, allowing for consistent and efficient checks across environments. While manual interpretation of the appropriateness of settings is necessary, the technical steps to gather the data can be automated, aligning with the second AI's suggestion for partial automation.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
ibm_openshift_garbage_collection_settings_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- IBM Cloud Resource/Service: OpenShift Container Platform
- API/CLI Method: Use `oc` CLI commands to automate retrieval of machine config pool details.
- Properties to Check: `eviction-soft`, `evictionSoftGracePeriod`, `evictionHard`, `evictionPressureTransitionPeriod`
- Pass Criteria: Settings are below the threshold of 12,500 terminated pods and aligned with system resources.
- Fail Criteria: Settings exceed the threshold or are not aligned with system resources.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
The first AI suggested a fully manual approach, while the second AI indicated potential for automation. By leveraging the OpenShift CLI, the retrieval and verification of settings can be automated, resolving the disagreement by focusing on the technical feasibility of automating the data collection process.

**SOURCE:** CIS_RedHat_OpenShift_Container_Platform_v4_Benchmark_v1.1.0_PDF_
